document.title: GUIDE FOR ASTM PIPING MATERIAL SPECIFICATION-- METALLIC PIPING.- ASTM Standards.
document.source_file_name: GUIDE FOR ASTM PIPING MATERIAL SPECIFICATION-- METALLIC PIPING.- ASTM Standards..pdf
document.conversion_purpose: Reusable JSON data for web app display and PDF-to-JSON comparison.
document.verification_design: The JSON contains both normalized structured rows and raw page-wise extracted layout text. For audit/display comparison, use source_pages[].text_layout and source_pages[].sha256 against the original PDF extraction.

schema.standard_note: All Material standards are as per ASTM and Dimensional Standards are as per ASME, as stated on page 6.

field_notes.material_specification_rows: Normalized table entries. Multi-line cells are represented as arrays.
field_notes.source_pages: Page-wise layout text extracted from the PDF text layer for comparison and traceability.

material_specification_rows.remarks: Carbon steel rows consolidated from A53/A106/A671/A672 and API 5L grades shown on page 1.

special_notes.text: Grades UNS N08367,N08904, N08925 & N08926 were previously covered under non-ferrous category, now recognized as Austenitic Stainless Steel.
Normal Grade has 0.08% C, Grade H has 0.04 to 0.1 % C and Grade L has 0.035% C and is of lower strength.

general_notes.text: A671 & A672 are plate fabricated pipes ≥ NPS16.Grade and Class as per plate mat. For A671 there are 28 Classes and for A672 there are 20 Classes.
Pipes to ASTM A 106 is widely used for services with moderate & high temperatures up to NPS 14 normally within the prescribed limit of temp.
ASME B 16.5 covers flanges from NPS ½ (DN15) to NPS 24 (DN600). For flanges from NPS 26 to NPS 60 refer ASME B 16.47. Select Series A or B
ASME B 16.11 covers Socket Weld / Screwed fittings up to NPS 4 (DN 100). Generally used only upto DN40.

ASME B 16.9 covers BW fittings up to NPS 48(DN1200). To specify fittings beyond this size, add suffix "S58" to the material spec. (See A960) If dimensional requirements to B16,9 are not exactly followed, then add suffix "S9" to ferrous and "SPLD" to non-ferrous fittings.( See Clause 4.4.2 ) MSS-SP-75 covers BW fittings from NPS 16 to NPS 60 in Carbon Steel and low & intermediate Alloy Steel. Up to NPS 14 refer ASME B16.9. MSS-SP-43 covers fittings of SS Grades 304,304L,316,316L &347 in NPS 1/2 to NPS24 in Sch. 5S & Sch. 10S thk. and are of "CR" category only.
Selection of grades to match mechanical strength with that of Fittings/Castings.
Pipes to A 369 can also be selected but it is a forged and bored pipe..

In case of Stainless Steel / Non-ferrous Fittings, Seamless construction to be specified as WPS and with Welded construction to be specified as WPW. If welds are UT tested, then the designation to be WPWU and if Radiographically tested it should be WPWX. No Class & Tests for CR Grades. In case of Carbon and Alloy Steels WP stands for Seamless ,add suffix W for welded quality and WU for Ultrasonic testing in lieu of Radiography..
A358 is plate fabricated pipe. For class refer standard. Pipes to A 409 can also be selected.A409 covers EFW pipes NPS 14 to 30 in Sch. 5S &10S thk.
A53 Gr. A ,API 5L Grade A25 and X42 and above are not recommended by Code to be used above 400º F
A 350 Gr. LF787 Cl.2 is up to -75°F(-59°C) and Cl.3 is up to -100°F(-73°C) whereas A 333 Gr. 4 is suitable for -150°F to 650 oF(-100°C to 345oC)
A 335 Gr. P5 has three grades P5, P5b and P5c, A182 Gr.F11& A234 WP11has 3, F12./WP12 has 2 & F22/WP22 has 2 Classes each.
Aluminum can be used for Cryogenic Service up to — 452°F(—270°C)and Austenitic Stainless Steel up to — 425°F(—255°C)
B 337 covers pipe sizes up to NPS 12 (DN 300) and B 861/ 862 cover sizes up to NPS 36 (DN900)
B407 & B 466 cover pipes up to NPS 6 (DN150) only.

For API 5L, two Product Specification Levels - PSL-1 & PSL-2 & Grades are A25, A, B, X42, X46, X52, X56, X60, X65, X70, X80, X90, X100 & X120. PSL-1 is available in Grades fro A25 to X70 and PSL-2 from Gr.B to X120. Gr.A25 is limited to NPS 5.Through Flow Line (TFL) pipes are furnished in double random lengths.
To be impact tested to the specified Minimum Temperature. Gr.10 (—75ºF) & 11(—320ºF) are listed in A333, but not adapted by B31.3 yet.
Materials grades listed in ASME B 31.3 are mainly considered along with other popular grades in this Chart. Though other grades are also available as . per the Material Standards.
Grade from Casting Specification is selected to match the mechanical strength of the pipe with the cast component
ASTM A 890 is the Duplex SS casting for General application and A 995 is that for pressure containing parts.
Heat Treatment not mandatory for flanges in Class 150 & 300.When heat treatment to be done by normalizing, material is designated as 105N.
For the selection of materials for Hydrogen service at high temperature & high pressure, use NELSON Curve in API RP941.

For selection of Piping materials for Sweet / Sour service, requirements outlined in NACE standard MR 0175/ 0103 shall be followed. To select MOC for Caustic Service refer 'Caustic Service Chart' published by NACE in SP 0403 & for Concentrated H2SO4, refer NACE RP0391
Titanium and Aluminum are not listed in ASME B16.5. Use Appendix A to establish flange rating.

Notes:-        ( All Material standards are as per ASTM and Dimensional Standards are as per ASME.)
1) A671 & A672 are plate fabricated pipes ≥ NPS16.Grade and Class as per plate mat. For A671 there are 28 Classes and for A672 there are 20 Classes.
2) Pipes to ASTM A 106 is widely used for services with moderate & high temperatures up to NPS 14 normally within the prescribed limit of temp.
3) ASME B 16.5 covers flanges from NPS ½ (DN15) to NPS 24 (DN600). For flanges from NPS 26 to NPS 60 refer ASME B 16.47. Select Series A or B
4) ASME B 16.11 covers Socket Weld / Screwed fittings up to NPS 4 (DN 100). Generally used only upto DN40.
5) ASME B 16.9 covers BW fittings up to NPS 48(DN1200). To specify fittings beyond this size, add suffix "S58" to the material spec. (See A960)
   If dimensional requirements to B16,9 are not exactly followed, then add suffix "S9" to ferrous and "SPLD" to non-ferrous fittings.( See Clause 4.4.2 )
   MSS-SP-75 covers BW fittings from NPS 16 to NPS 60 in Carbon Steel and low & intermediate Alloy Steel. Up to NPS 14 refer ASME B16.9.
   MSS-SP-43 covers fittings of SS Grades 304,304L,316,316L &347 in NPS 1/2 to NPS24 in Sch. 5S & Sch. 10S thk. and are of "CR" category only.
6) Selection of grades to match mechanical strength with that of Fittings/Castings.
7) Pipes to A 369 can also be selected but it is a forged and bored pipe..
8) In case of Stainless Steel / Non-ferrous Fittings, Seamless construction to be specified as WPS and with Welded construction to be specified as WPW.
 If welds are UT tested, then the designation to be WPWU and if Radiographically tested it should be WPWX. No Class & Tests for CR Grades.
 In case of Carbon and Alloy Steels WP stands for Seamless ,add suffix W for welded quality and WU for Ultrasonic testing in lieu of Radiography..
9) A358 is plate fabricated pipe. For class refer standard. Pipes to A 409 can also be selected.A409 covers EFW pipes NPS 14 to 30 in Sch. 5S &10S thk.
10) A53 Gr. A ,API 5L Grade A25 and X42 and above are not recommended by Code to be used above 400º F
11) A 350 Gr. LF787 Cl.2 is up to -75°F(-59°C) and Cl.3 is up to -100°F(-73°C) whereas A 333 Gr. 4 is suitable for -150°F to 650 oF(-100°C to 345oC)
12) A 335 Gr. P5 has three grades P5, P5b and P5c, A182 Gr.F11& A234 WP11has 3, F12./WP12 has 2 & F22/WP22 has 2 Classes each.
13) Aluminum can be used for Cryogenic Service up to — 452°F(—270°C)and Austenitic Stainless Steel up to — 425°F(—255°C)
14).B 337 covers pipe sizes up to NPS 12 (DN 300) and B 861/ 862 cover sizes up to NPS 36 (DN900)
15) B407 & B 466 cover pipes up to NPS 6 (DN150) only.
16) For API 5L, two Product Specification Levels - PSL-1 & PSL-2 & Grades are A25, A, B, X42, X46, X52, X56, X60, X65, X70, X80, X90, X100 & X120.
   PSL-1 is available in Grades fro A25 to X70 and PSL-2 from Gr.B to X120. Gr.A25 is limited to NPS 5.Through Flow Line (TFL) pipes are furnished in
  double random lengths.
17) To be impact tested to the specified Minimum Temperature. Gr.10 (—75ºF) & 11(—320ºF) are listed in A333, but not adapted by B31.3 yet.
18) Materials grades listed in ASME B 31.3 are mainly considered along with other popular grades in this Chart. Though other grades are also available as .
  per the Material Standards.
19) Grade from Casting Specification is selected to match the mechanical strength of the pipe with the cast component
20) ASTM A 890 is the Duplex SS casting for General application and A 995 is that for pressure containing parts.
21) Heat Treatment not mandatory for flanges in Class 150 & 300.When heat treatment to be done by normalizing, material is designated as 105N.
22) For the selection of materials for Hydrogen service at high temperature & high pressure, use NELSON Curve in API RP941.
23) For selection of Piping materials for Sweet / Sour service, requirements outlined in NACE standard MR 0175/ 0103 shall be followed.
  To select MOC for Caustic Service refer 'Caustic Service Chart' published by NACE in SP 0403 & for Concentrated H2SO4, refer NACE RP0391
24) Titanium and Aluminum are not listed in ASME B16.5. Use Appendix A to establish flange rating.
